args.code:
import json
import re

# Read the full business data from the file
file_path = None
for key, value in locals().items():
    if isinstance(value, str) and value.endswith('.json'):
        file_path = value
        break

if file_path:
    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    # Fallback to the preview data
    businesses = [
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ab", "business_id": "businessid_49", "name": "Steps to Learning Montessori Preschool", "review_count": "8", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 6901 Phelps Rd in Goleta, CA, this facility offers a nurturing environment for young learners, providing a range of services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ac", "business_id": "businessid_47", "name": "Breeze Blow Dry Bar", "review_count": "81", "is_open": "0", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 9916 Clayton Rd in St. Louis, MO, this establishment offers a wide range of services, including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists, Skin Care, Blow Dry/Out Services, and Makeup Artists."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ad", "business_id": "businessid_88", "name": "Impact Guns", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 11655 W Executive Dr in Boise, ID, this facility offers enthusiasts a premier destination for Gun/Rifle Ranges, Active Life."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2af", "business_id": "businessid_33", "name": "J&Q Nails", "review_count": "28", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 9655 E US Hwy 36, Unit H in Avon, IN, this establishment offers a range of services including Nail Salons, Hair Removal, Beauty & Spas, and Waxing."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2b1", "business_id": "businessid_92", "name": "Luminosity", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 690 W Dekalb Pike in King of Prussia, PA, this business offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of Cosmetics & Beauty Supply, Cosmetic Dentists, Tanning, Teeth Whitening, Beauty & Spas, Dentists, Shopping, Blow Dry/Out Services, Health & Medical, Spray Tanning, and Hair Salons."}
    ]

# Extract categories from descriptions
category_count = {}
credit_card_businesses_by_category = {}

for business in businesses:
    # Check if accepts credit cards
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attributes, str) and attributes == 'None':
        continue
    
    accepts_cc = False
    if isinstance(attributes, dict):
        accepts_cc = attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True'
    
    if not accepts_cc:
        continue
    
    # Extract categories from description
    description = business.get('description', '')
    if not description:
        continue
    
    # Find phrases after "in" or "including" or just look for category list patterns
    # Pattern 1: "in Category1, Category2, and Category3."
    # Pattern 2: "including Category1, Category2, and Category3."
    # Pattern 3: "services in Category1, Category2, Category3."
    
    categories = []
    
    # Try different patterns
    patterns = [
        r'in ([^.]+)\.',
        r'including ([^.]+)\.',
        r'services in ([^.]+)\.',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)\.'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by commas and "and"
            cat_list = re.split(r',\s*|\sand\s', cat_text)
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in cat_list if cat.strip()]
            break
    
    # If no pattern matched, try to extract from "category of" pattern
    if not categories:
        match = re.search(r"category of '([^']+)'", description)
        if match:
            categories = [match.group(1).strip()]
    
    # Count businesses per category
    for category in categories:
        category_count[category] = category_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        
        # Store business info by category
        if category not in credit_card_businesses_by_category:
            credit_card_businesses_by_category[category] = []
        credit_card_businesses_by_category[category].append({
            'business_id': business['business_id'],
            'business_ref': business['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_'),
            'name': business['name']
        })

# Find category with most businesses
sorted_categories = sorted(category_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

categories_info = {
    'sorted_categories': sorted_categories,
    'businesses_by_category': credit_card_businesses_by_category
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(categories_info))

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Read the full business data from the file
file_path = None
for key, value in locals().items():
    if isinstance(value, str) and value.endswith('.json'):
        file_path = value
        break

if file_path:
    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    # Fallback to the preview data
    businesses = [
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ab", "business_id": "businessid_49", "name": "Steps to Learning Montessori Preschool", "review_count": "8", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 6901 Phelps Rd in Goleta, CA, this facility offers a nurturing environment for young learners, providing a range of services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ac", "business_id": "businessid_47", "name": "Breeze Blow Dry Bar", "review_count": "81", "is_open": "0", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 9916 Clayton Rd in St. Louis, MO, this establishment offers a wide range of services, including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists, Skin Care, Blow Dry/Out Services, and Makeup Artists."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ad", "business_id": "businessid_88", "name": "Impact Guns", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 11655 W Executive Dr in Boise, ID, this facility offers enthusiasts a premier destination for Gun/Rifle Ranges, Active Life."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2af", "business_id": "businessid_33", "name": "J&Q Nails", "review_count": "28", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 9655 E US Hwy 36, Unit H in Avon, IN, this establishment offers a range of services including Nail Salons, Hair Removal, Beauty & Spas, and Waxing."},
        {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2b1", "business_id": "businessid_92", "name": "Luminosity", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True"}, "description": "Located at 690 W Dekalb Pike in King of Prussia, PA, this business offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of Cosmetics & Beauty Supply, Cosmetic Dentists, Tanning, Teeth Whitening, Beauty & Spas, Dentists, Shopping, Blow Dry/Out Services, Health & Medical, Spray Tanning, and Hair Salons."}
    ]

# Extract categories from descriptions
category_count = {}
credit_card_businesses_by_category = {}

for business in businesses:
    # Check if accepts credit cards
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attributes, str) and attributes == 'None':
        continue
    
    accepts_cc = False
    if isinstance(attributes, dict):
        accepts_cc = attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True'
    
    if not accepts_cc:
        continue
    
    # Extract categories from description
    description = business.get('description', '')
    if not description:
        continue
    
    # Find phrases after "in" or "including" or just look for category list patterns
    # Pattern 1: "in Category1, Category2, and Category3."
    # Pattern 2: "including Category1, Category2, and Category3."
    # Pattern 3: "services in Category1, Category2, Category3."
    
    categories = []
    
    # Try different patterns
    patterns = [
        r'in ([^.]+)\.',
        r'including ([^.]+)\.',
        r'services in ([^.]+)\.',
        r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+)\.'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by commas and "and"
            cat_list = re.split(r',\s*|\sand\s', cat_text)
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in cat_list if cat.strip()]
            break
    
    # If no pattern matched, try to extract from "category of" pattern
    if not categories:
        match = re.search(r"category of '([^']+)'", description)
        if match:
            categories = [match.group(1).strip()]
    
    # Count businesses per category
    for category in categories:
        category_count[category] = category_count.get(category, 0) + 1
        
        # Store business info by category
        if category not in credit_card_businesses_by_category:
            credit_card_businesses_by_category[category] = []
        credit_card_businesses_by_category[category].append({
            'business_id': business['business_id'],
            'business_ref': business['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_'),
            'name': business['name']
        })

# Find category with most businesses
sorted_categories = sorted(category_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

categories_info = {
    'sorted_categories': sorted_categories,
    'businesses_by_category': credit_card_businesses_by_category
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(categories_info))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Read the full business data from the file
file_path = None
for key, value in locals().items():
    if isinstance(value, str) and value.endswith('.json'):
        file_path = value
        break

# Read the data
if file_path:
    with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    # Use the preview data we have
    all_businesses = var_functions.query_db_14

# Process all businesses to find those accepting credit cards and their categories
credit_card_businesses = []
cycling_businesses = {}  # bikes or cycling related
category_businesses = {}  # all categories

for business in all_businesses:
    # Check if accepts credit cards
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attributes, str) and attributes == 'None':
        continue
    
    accepts_cc = False
    if isinstance(attributes, dict):
        accepts_cc = attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True'
    
    if not accepts_cc:
        continue
    
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    business_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    name = business.get('name', '')
    
    # Save business info
    credit_card_businesses.append({
        'business_id': business_id,
        'business_ref': business_ref,
        'name': name
    })
    
    # Check if bike cycling related
    name_lower = name.lower()
    if 'bike' in name_lower or 'cycle' in name_lower or 'bicycle' in name_lower:
        cycling_businesses[name] = business_ref
    
    # Extract categories from description
    description = business.get('description', '')
    if description:
        # Try to extract categories from description
        categories = []
        
        # Pattern: "in Category1, Category2, and Category3."
        patterns = [
            r'in ([^.]+?)\.',
            r'including ([^.]+?)\.',
            r'services in ([^.]+?)\.',
            r'offers a range of services including ([^.]+?)\.',
            r'category of \'([^\']+)\''
        ]
        
        for pattern in patterns:
            match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                cat_text = match.group(1)
                # Split by commas and "and"
                cat_list = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+', cat_text)
                for cat in cat_list:
                    cat_clean = cat.strip()
                    if cat_clean and cat_clean not in ['Local Services', 'Health & Medical', 'Shopping', 'Food', 'Beauty & Spas']:
                        categories.append(cat_clean)
                if categories:
                    break
        
        # If no pattern matched, use broader categories
        if not categories:
            if 'Restaurant' in description or 'Food' in description:
                categories.append('Restaurants')
            if 'Salon' in description or 'Beauty' in description:
                categories.append('Beauty & Spas')
            if 'Medical' in description or 'Doctor' in description or 'Health' in description:
                categories.append('Health & Medical')
        
        # Count businesses per category
        for category in categories:
            if category not in category_businesses:
                category_businesses[category] = []
            category_businesses[category].append({
                'business_id': business_id,
                'business_ref': business_ref,
                'name': name
            })

# Find category with most businesses accepting credit cards
sorted_categories = sorted(category_businesses.items(), key=lambda x: len(x[1]), reverse=True)

# Get top category
top_category = None
top_count = 0
if sorted_categories:
    top_category, businesses = sorted_categories[0]
    top_count = len(businesses)
    print(f"Top category: {top_category} with {top_count} businesses")
    print(f"Category ranking: {[(cat, len(buss)) for cat, buss in sorted_categories[:10]]}")
    
    # Get business refs for top category
    business_refs = [b['business_ref'] for b in businesses]
    print(f"Business refs count: {len(business_refs)}")
else:
    print("No categories found")
    # Let's check bike names as fallback
    if cycling_businesses:
        top_category = "Bicycle"
        business_refs = list(cycling_businesses.values())
        print(f"Using bike shops as fallback: {len(business_refs)}")
    else:
        # Use all credit card businesses
        top_category = "All Businesses"
        business_refs = [b['business_ref'] for b in credit_card_businesses]
        print(f"Using all businesses: {len(business_refs)}")

# Prepare result
result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': len(business_refs) if 'business_refs' in locals() else 0,
    'business_refs': business_refs if 'business_refs' in locals() else []
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

result.preview: "Execution failed with exit code 1\nNameError: name 'var_functions' is not defined"

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get businesses that accept credit cards
businesses = var_functions.query_db_16

# Extract categories and count businesses per category
category_businesses = {}

for business in businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    business_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern matching for list of categories
    patterns = [
        r'in ([^.]+)\\.',
        r'including ([^.]+)\\.',
        r'services in ([^.]+)\\.',
        r'category of \\'([^\\']+)\\''
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            cat_parts = re.split(r',\\s*|\\sand\\s', cat_text)
            categories = [cat.strip() for cat in cat_parts if cat.strip()]
            break
    
    # Classification for known patterns
    if not categories:
        if 'Beauty' in desc or 'Salon' in desc or 'Spa' in desc:
            categories = ['Beauty & Spas']
        elif 'Medical' in desc or 'Doctor' in desc or 'Health' in desc:
            categories = ['Health & Medical']
        elif 'Restaurant' in desc or 'Food' in desc:
            categories = ['Restaurants']
        elif 'Shopping' in desc:
            categories = ['Shopping']
        elif 'Education' in desc:
            categories = ['Education']
    
    for category in categories:
        category_businesses[category] = category_businesses.get(category, [])
        category_businesses[category].append(business_ref)

# Find top category
top_category = max(category_businesses.keys(), key=lambda k: len(category_businesses[k])) if category_businesses else 'Unknown'
top_business_refs = category_businesses.get(top_category, [])

result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': len(top_business_refs),
    'business_refs': top_business_refs
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the credit card businesses from the latest query
file_path = var_functions.query_db_34

with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    cc_businesses = json.load(f)

# Extract categories and count businesses per category
category_counts = {}
category_businesses = {}

for business in cc_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    business_id = business.get('business_id', '')
    business_ref = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    name = business.get('name', '')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Try to extract categories from description patterns
    patterns = [
        (r'in ([^.]+?)\.', ''),
        (r'including ([^.]+?)\.', ''),
        (r'services in ([^.]+?)\.', ''),
        (r'category of [\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]', '')
    ]
    
    found = False
    for pattern, _ in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            # Split by commas and "and"
            cat_parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+', cat_text)
            for part in cat_parts:
                part = part.strip()
                if part and len(part) > 2 and part not in ['a range of services', 'the fields of']:
                    # Clean up
                    part = part.replace(' in the fields of', '').strip()
                    categories.append(part)
            found = True
            break
    
    # If no categories found, classify by keywords
    if not categories:
        name_lower = name.lower()
        desc_lower = description.lower()
        
        if 'nail' in name_lower or 'salon' in name_lower or 'beauty' in desc_lower or 'spa' in desc_lower:
            categories = ['Beauty & Spas']
        elif 'restaurant' in name_lower or 'food' in desc_lower or 'cafe' in name_lower:
            categories = ['Restaurants']
        elif 'medical' in desc_lower or 'doctor' in desc_lower or 'health' in desc_lower:
            categories = ['Health & Medical']
        elif 'antique' in desc_lower or 'florist' in desc_lower or 'shopping' in desc_lower:
            categories = ['Shopping']
        elif 'preschool' in desc_lower or 'education' in desc_lower or 'school' in name_lower:
            categories = ['Education']
        elif 'gun' in desc_lower or 'range' in name_lower:
            categories = ['Active Life']
        elif 'transportation' in desc_lower or 'taxi' in desc_lower:
            categories = ['Transportation']
        else:
            categories = ['Other']
    
    for category in categories:
        category = category.strip()
        if category:
            category_counts[category] = category_counts.get(category, 0) + 1
            if category not in category_businesses:
                category_businesses[category] = []
            category_businesses[category].append(business_ref)

# Find the category with the most businesses
if category_counts:
    top_category = max(category_counts.keys(), key=lambda k: category_counts[k])
    top_business_count = category_counts[top_category]
    top_business_refs = category_businesses[top_category]
else:
    top_category = 'Unknown'
    top_business_count = 0
    top_business_refs = []

result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_business_count,
    'business_refs': top_business_refs,
    'category_breakdown': category_counts
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

        elif 'preschool' in desc_lower or 'education' in desc_lower or 'school' in name_lower:
            categories = ['Education']
        elif 'gun' in desc_lower or 'range' in name_lower:
            categories = ['Active Life']
        elif 'transportation' in desc_lower or 'taxi' in desc_lower:
            categories = ['Transportation']
        else:
            categories = ['Other']
    
    for category in categories:
        category = category.strip()
        if category:
            category_counts[category] = category_counts.get(category, 0) + 1
            if category not in category_businesses:
                category_businesses[category] = []
            category_businesses[category].append(business_ref)

# Find the category with the most businesses
if category_counts:
    top_category = max(category_counts.keys(), key=lambda k: category_counts[k])
    top_business_count = category_counts[top_category]
    top_business_refs = category_businesses[top_category]
else:
    top_category = 'Unknown'
    top_business_count = 0
    top_business_refs = []

result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_business_count,
    'business_refs': top_business_refs,
    'category_breakdown': category_counts
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

result.preview: "Execution failed with exit code 1\nNameError: name 'var_functions' is not defined"

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load credit card businesses
cc_file = var_functions.query_db_42
with open(cc_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print('Processing', len(businesses), 'businesses')

# Count by category
from collections import defaultdict
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
category_refs = defaultdict(list)

for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    biz_ref = biz_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    name = biz.get('name', '')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Extract categories from description
    # Simple pattern: find text after "in" until period
    m = re.search(r'in ([^.]+)\.', desc)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Clean
        text = text.replace(' the fields of ', '').replace(' a range of services ', '').replace(' a diverse ', '')
        text = text.replace(' a delightful ', '').replace(' your home and decorative needs', '')
        # Split
        parts = re.split(r',\s*| and |&', text)
        for p in parts:
            p = p.strip()
            if p and len(p) > 2 and p not in ['Local Services', 'Health & Medical', 'Shopping', 'Food']:
                categories.append(p)
    
    # Keyword classification
    if not categories:
        d = desc.lower()
        n = name.lower()
        
        if 'nail' in n or 'salon' in n or 'spa' in n:
            categories = ['Beauty & Spa']
        elif 'restaurant' in n or 'cafe' in n or 'food' in d:
            categories = ['Restaurant']
        elif 'gun' in n or 'active life' in d:
            categories = ['Active Life']
        elif 'florist' in n or 'floral' in n:
            categories = ['Florist']
        elif 'medical' in n or 'care' in n:
            categories = ['Health & Medical']
        elif 'preschool' in d or 'education' in d:
            categories = ['Education']
        elif 'antique' in n:
            categories = ['Antiques']
        elif 'transportation' in d or 'uber' in n:
            categories = ['Transportation']
        elif 'convenience' in d:
            categories = ['Convenience Store']
        else:
            categories = ['Other']
    
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip()
        if cat:
            category_counts[cat] += 1
            category_refs[cat].append(biz_ref)

# Find top category
if category_counts:
    top_cat = max(category_counts.keys(), key=lambda k: category_counts[k])
    top_count = category_counts[top_cat]
    top_refs = category_refs[top_cat]
else:
    top_cat = 'Unknown'
    top_count = 0
    top_refs = []

result = {
    'top_category': top_cat,
    'top_count': top_count,
    'top_business_refs': top_refs[:10]  # First 10 for now
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load credit card businesses
cc_file = var_functions.query_db_42
with open(cc_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

print('Processing', len(businesses), 'businesses')

# Count by category
from collections import defaultdict
category_counts = defaultdict(int)
category_refs = defaultdict(list)

for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    biz_ref = biz_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    name = biz.get('name', '')
    
    categories = []
    
    # Extract categories from description
    # Simple pattern: find text after "in" until period
    m = re.search(r'in ([^.]+)\.', desc)
    if m:
        text = m.group(1)
        # Clean
        text = text.replace(' the fields of ', '').replace(' a range of services ', '').replace(' a diverse ', '')
        text = text.replace(' a delightful ', '').replace(' your home and decorative needs', '')
        # Split
        parts = re.split(r',\s*| and |&', text)
        for p in parts:
            p = p.strip()
            if p and len(p) > 2 and p not in ['Local Services', 'Health & Medical', 'Shopping', 'Food']:
                categories.append(p)
    
    # Keyword classification
    if not categories:
        d = desc.lower()
        n = name.lower()
        
        if 'nail' in n or 'salon' in n or 'spa' in n:
            categories = ['Beauty & Spa']
        elif 'restaurant' in n or 'cafe' in n or 'food' in d:
            categories = ['Restaurant']
        elif 'gun' in n or 'active life' in d:
            categories = ['Active Life']
        elif 'florist' in n or 'floral' in n:
            categories = ['Florist']
        elif 'medical' in n or 'care' in n:
            categories = ['Health & Medical']
        elif 'preschool' in d or 'education' in d:
            categories = ['Education']
        elif 'antique' in n:
            categories = ['Antiques']
        elif 'transportation' in d or 'uber' in n:
            categories = ['Transportation']
        elif 'convenience' in d:
            categories = ['Convenience Store']
        else:
            categories = ['Other']
    
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip()
        if cat:
            category_counts[cat] += 1
            category_refs[cat].append(biz_ref)

# Find top category
if category_counts:
    top_cat = max(category_counts.keys(), key=lambda k: category_counts[k])
    top_count = category_counts[top_cat]
    top_refs = category_refs[top_cat]
else:
    top_cat = 'Unknown'
    top_count = 0
    top_refs = []

result = {
    'top_category': top_cat,
    'top_count': top_count,
    'top_business_refs': top_refs[:10]  # First 10 for now
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get the list of variables in locals
print('Available variables:', list(locals().keys()))

# Find the query result variable
cc_businesses = []
for key, value in locals().items():
    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) > 0 and value[0].get('description'):

        break

print(f'Found {len(cc_businesses)} businesses')

# Just show the first few descriptions to understand the format
for i, biz in enumerate(cc_businesses[:3]):
    print(f"Business {i+1}: {biz['description'][:100]}...")

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'count': len(cc_businesses)}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
import re

# Load credit card businesses from the file
cc_file = var_functions.query_db_84
with open(cc_file, 'r') as f:
    cc_businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Found {len(cc_businesses)} businesses that accept credit cards')

# Extract first 10 to show the structure
for i, biz in enumerate(cc_businesses[:3]):
    print(f"Business {i+1}: {biz['name'][:50]} - {biz['description'][:100]}...")

# Simple category extraction for now
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'count': len(cc_businesses)}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load credit card businesses from the file
cc_file = var_functions.query_db_84
with open(cc_file, 'r') as f:
    cc_businesses = json.load(f)

print(f'Found {len(cc_businesses)} businesses that accept credit cards')

# Extract first 10 to show the structure
for i, biz in enumerate(cc_businesses[:3]):
    print(f"Business {i+1}: {biz['name'][:50]} - {biz['description'][:100]}...")

# Simple category extraction for now
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'count': len(cc_businesses)}))

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:18.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
